title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Haemul Pajeon: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Haemul Pajeon with our easy recipe! Learn to make crispy Korean seafood pancakes packed with flavor. Perfect for a delicious meal at home.
longTailKeywords: best Haemul Pajeon recipe at home
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake)?
Step-by-Step Guide to Making Haemul Pajeon
Tips for the Crispiest Korean Seafood Pancake
Serving Suggestions for Your Haemul Pajeon
Variations and Substitutions for Haemul Pajeon
seo.imageAlt: A freshly cooked, crispy Haemul Pajeon, a traditional Korean seafood pancake, garnished with scallions and served with a dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the savory delight of Haemul Pajeon, a beloved Korean seafood pancake perfect for any occasion.

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, celebrated for its crispy edges and tender interior. Packed with an array of fresh seafood and vibrant scallions, each bite offers a delightful burst of oceanic flavor. This comforting dish is perfect as an appetizer or a light meal, often enjoyed with a tangy dipping sauce.

ingredients.notes: shrimp, squid, mussels, clams, chopped if large
thinly sliced, for a bit of heat

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Thoroughly rinse the assorted seafood and pat them dry. If any pieces are large, chop them into bite-sized portions. Prepare the scallions by cutting them into 3-inch lengths. Thinly slice the yellow onion and green chili, if using. Set all prepared ingredients aside.

instructions.title: Mix the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, cornstarch, and salt. Gradually add the cold water and the lightly beaten egg, mixing until just combined. Be careful not to overmix; a few lumps are perfectly fine and will result in a lighter, crispier pancake. The batter should be slightly thick, similar to a thin pancake batter.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients with Batter
instructions.text: Gently fold the prepared seafood, scallions, sliced onion, and green chili into the batter. Ensure all ingredients are well coated but avoid mashing them. This mixture forms the base for your savory pancake.

instructions.title: Heat the Pan
instructions.text: Heat a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add a generous amount of vegetable oil (about 2-3 tablespoons) to coat the bottom of the pan. Ensure the oil is hot but not smoking, creating a shimmering surface.

instructions.title: Cook the Pancake
instructions.text: Pour about half of the batter mixture into the hot pan, spreading it evenly to form a round pancake, about 8-10 inches in diameter. You may need to use a spoon to arrange the seafood and scallions nicely on top. Cook for about 4-5 minutes on one side until the bottom is golden brown and crispy.

instructions.title: Flip and Finish Cooking
instructions.text: Carefully flip the pancake using a large spatula. Add a little more oil around the edges if needed to promote crispiness. Continue to cook for another 4-5 minutes on the second side, or until it is golden brown, crispy, and the seafood is fully cooked through. Repeat with the remaining batter for a second pancake.

instructions.title: Prepare Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: While the pancakes are cooking, combine soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and sesame seeds in a small bowl to create a simple yet flavorful dipping sauce. Adjust to your taste preferences.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board and slice it into wedges. Serve hot with the prepared dipping sauce on the side. Enjoy this crispy, savory Korean seafood delight as an appetizer or a light meal.

tips: Use very cold water for the batter to achieve a crispier pancake; some even add a few ice cubes to the water.
Do not overmix the batter; a few lumps are fine and contribute to a lighter, more tender texture.
For an extra crispy pancake, use a cast-iron skillet and a generous amount of oil, ensuring the pan is hot before adding the batter.
article: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake is a beloved Korean Seafood dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
